  1. Description
  2. Method Summary

    1. equalsModThisProperty(T, T, V...)
    2. hashCodeModThisProperty(T)

Interface Property<T>

Type Parameters:
T - the type of the objects that are checked for equality or hashed
All Known Implementing Classes:
IrrelevantTermLabelsProperty, ProofIrrelevancyProperty, RenamingSourceElementProperty, RenamingTermProperty, TermLabelsProperty
public interface Property<T>

This interface is used for equality checks and hashing modulo a certain property.

Objects of classes implementing this interface are given to the methods in Property as parameters to unify equality checks and hashing modulo different properties.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    <V> boolean
    equalsModThisProperty(T t1, T t2, V... v)
    Checks t1 and t2 for equality ignoring a certain property.
    int
    hashCodeModThisProperty(T t)
    Computes the hash code of t in a context where [#equalsModThisProperty(Object,Object,Object[])] is used as an equality check, so that it can be used in, e.g., a HashMap.

  • Method Details

    • equalsModThisProperty

      <V> boolean equalsModThisProperty(T t1, T t2, V... v)
      Checks t1 and t2 for equality ignoring a certain property.
      Type Parameters:
      V - the type of the additional parameters needed for the comparison
      Parameters:
      t1 - the first element of the equality check
      t2 - the second element of the equality check
      v - the additional arguments needed for the equality check
      Returns:
      whether t1 and t2 are equal ignoring a certain property

    • hashCodeModThisProperty

      int hashCodeModThisProperty(T t)
      Computes the hash code of t in a context where [#equalsModThisProperty(Object,Object,Object[])] is used as an equality check, so that it can be used in, e.g., a HashMap.
      Parameters:
      t - the object to compute the hash code for
      Returns:
      the hash code of t ignoring a certain property
